appeal_letter:
To Whom It May Concern,

I am appealing the denial of prior authorization for a laparoscopic excision surgery with Dr. Jane Doe as an out-of-network service, submitted as a Network Gap Exception request.

The denial states that out-of-network services require a Network Gap Exception proving that no in-network provider within 30 miles is capable of performing the requested service. It cites three in-network OB/GYNs in my area who perform laparoscopic surgery, and concludes that because I have not demonstrated that these providers are clinically unable to treat my condition, the exception is denied.

I am addressing that stated criterion directly. The standard is whether the in-network providers are clinically capable of performing the requested service for my condition, and the documented facts show that they are not. My condition is severe, deeply infiltrating endometriosis involving the bowel and ureters. The in-network OB/GYNs identified in the directory perform only simple ablation. Ablation has already failed twice in my case, and it is contraindicated for deep bowel involvement such as mine. The requested service is laparoscopic excision performed by an excision specialist, which is a different procedure addressing the disease the listed providers do not treat. Listing providers who perform laparoscopic surgery in general does not establish that they are capable of performing the specific excision required here. Applied to these individualized clinical facts, the plan's own capability criterion supports the requested out-of-network exception rather than the denial.

If the determination rests on the in-network providers being able to perform laparoscopic surgery generally, that is not the relevant question under the criterion cited; the question is whether they can perform the requested service for my documented condition, and the record indicates they cannot.

I also note that this denial does not state the deadline for filing an appeal or describe the process for requesting a full and fair review. I assert my right to a complete and fair review of this decision and request that the missing procedural information be provided.

I request that the denial be overturned and that the Network Gap Exception and prior authorization for laparoscopic excision surgery with Dr. Jane Doe be approved. I also request written confirmation of the appeal deadline and the process for further review.

Not legal or medical advice. Draft assistance only.

Respectfully,
Member
missing_evidence_checklist: Medical records documenting the two prior failed ablation procedures
A treating-provider letter stating that ablation is contraindicated for deep bowel and ureteral involvement
Documentation confirming the three listed in-network OB/GYNs perform only ablation and not deep infiltrating excision
Confirmation of the plan's appeal deadline and the procedure for a full and fair review
A copy of the plan's Network Gap Exception criteria language
